Title: A wearable electrochemical patch for continuous cortisol monitoring in sweat
Abstract: We present a flexible skin-worn electrochemical biosensor that measures cortisol in sweat during exercise. A molecularly imprinted polymer electrode provides selective, real-time detection of the stress hormone without invasive sampling.

Title: Non-invasive glucose sensing in tears with a soft contact-lens biosensor
Abstract: A contact-lens-integrated amperometric sensor tracks glucose in tear fluid. The device offers a needle-free route to blood-sugar estimation and demonstrates stable readings over a full day of wear.

Title: Real-time lactate measurement in sweat for exercise physiology
Abstract: This study reports a wearable enzymatic biosensor that quantifies lactate, a marker of muscle fatigue, in perspiration. Continuous lactate readings correlate with exercise intensity in a small pilot cohort.

Title: Potentiometric detection of sodium and potassium electrolytes in sweat
Abstract: A textile-based potentiometric sensor array simultaneously measures sodium and potassium in sweat, enabling hydration and electrolyte monitoring for athletes during physical activity.

Title: A flexible uric acid biosensor for wearable gout monitoring in sweat
Abstract: We describe an electrochemical wearable sensor for uric acid in sweat. The gout-associated analyte is detected with high selectivity, supporting at-home management of hyperuricemia.
Keywords: uric acid; sweat; wearable; electrochemical; gout

Title: Wearable sensors for sweat-based health monitoring: an overview
Abstract: This overview summarizes recent wearable biosensors that detect biomarkers such as glucose, lactate, cortisol, sodium, and uric acid in sweat, and discusses electrochemical and optical sensing principles for non-invasive health monitoring.
